Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me compile a comprehensive report:
---
# Test Infrastructure Guide for `erk reconcile` CLI Command
## 1. Test File Location & Imports Pattern
Based on `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/test_wt_checkout.py`:
```python """Tests for erk reconcile command."""
from pathlib import Path from click.testing import CliRunner
from erk.cli.cli import cli from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo, BranchSyncInfo from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_inmem_env ```
## 2. Test Context Builder Function
**Location**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/test_utils/context_builders.py`
**Signature**: ```python def build_workspace_test_context( env: ErkInMemEnv | ErkIsolatedFsEnv, *, dry_run: bool = False, use_graphite: bool = False, current_branch: str | None = None, **kwargs, ) -> ErkContext ```
**Key Features**: - Automatically creates `FakeGit` with sensible defaults if not provided in `kwargs` - Automatically sets up remote URLs for repo discovery - Provides default `FakeGitHub()` and `FakeShell()` if not in kwargs - Returns fully configured `ErkContext`
**Usage Pattern**: ```python with erk_inmem_env(runner) as env: ctx = build_workspace_test_context( env, current_branch="feature-1", git=custom_git_ops, # Optional override github=custom_github_ops # Optional override ) ```
## 3. FakeGitHub Constructor & Key Methods
**Location**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/fake.py`
**Constructor Signature** (relevant params only): ```python def __init__( self, *, repo_info: RepoInfo | None = None, prs: dict[str, PullRequestInfo] | None = None, # branch -> PullRequestInfo pr_details: dict[int, PRDetails] | None = None, # pr_number -> PRDetails prs_by_branch: dict[str, PRDetails] | None = None, # branch -> PRDetails (simpler) merge_should_succeed: bool = True, authenticated: bool = True, # ... many more params ) -> None ```
**Key Method - `get_pr_for_branch`**: ```python def get_pr_for_branch(self, repo_root: Path, branch: str) -> PRDetails | PRNotFound: """Get comprehensive PR details for a branch.
Checks prs_by_branch first (simpler lookup), then falls back to prs + pr_details (original two-step lookup). """ # Simple lookup first if branch in self._prs_by_branch: return self._prs_by_branch[branch]
# Fallback: legacy two-step lookup if branch not in self._prs: return PRNotFound()
pr_number = self._prs[branch].number if pr_number not in self._pr_details: return PRNotFound()
return self._pr_details[pr_number] ```
**Usage Example** from `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/land/test_core.py`: ```python github_ops = FakeGitHub( prs_by_branch={ "feature-1": PRDetails( number=123, url="https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123", title="Feature 1", body="PR body", state="OPEN", is_draft=False, base_ref_name="main", head_ref_name="feature-1", is_cross_repository=False, mergeable="MERGEABLE", merge_state_status="CLEAN", owner="owner", repo="repo", ) }, merge_should_succeed=True, ) ```
## 4. FakeGit (FakeGitBranchOps) Constructor
**Location**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/fake.py`
**Constructor Signature** (branch-related params): ```python def __init__( self, *, worktrees: dict[Path, list[WorktreeInfo]] | None = None, current_branches: dict[Path, str | None] | None = None, local_branches: dict[Path, list[str]] | None = None, remote_branches: dict[Path, list[str]] | None = None, branch_heads: dict[str, str] | None = None, trunk_branches: dict[Path, str] | None = None, ahead_behind: dict[tuple[Path, str], tuple[int, int]] | None = None, branch_divergence: dict[tuple[Path, str, str], BranchDivergence] | None = None, branch_sync_info: dict[Path, dict[str, BranchSyncInfo]] | None = None, behind_commit_authors: dict[tuple[Path, str], list[str]] | None = None, # ... more params ) -> None ```
**Key Parameters Explained**: - `branch_sync_info`: Maps `repo_root -> {branch_name -> BranchSyncInfo}` - `BranchSyncInfo` contains: `tracking_branch`, `ahead`, `behind`, `commit_authors` - `local_branches`: Maps `repo_root -> list[branch_name]` - `ahead_behind`: Maps `(cwd, branch) -> (ahead_count, behind_count)`
**Key Method - `list_local_branches`**: ```python def list_local_branches(self, repo_root: Path) -> list[str]: """List all local branches in the repository.""" return self._local_branches.get(repo_root, []) ```
## 5. FakeWorktree Constructor & Methods
**Location**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/worktree/fake.py`
**Constructor Signature**: ```python def __init__( self, *, worktrees: dict[Path, list[WorktreeInfo]] | None = None, existing_paths: set[Path] | None = None, dirty_worktrees: set[Path] | None = None, add_worktree_error: str | None = None, remove_worktree_error: str | None = None, ) -> None ```
**Key Method - `find_worktree_for_branch`**: ```python def find_worktree_for_branch(self, repo_root: Path, branch: str) -> Path | None: """Find worktree path for given branch name in fake data.""" worktrees = self.list_worktrees(repo_root) for wt in worktrees: if wt.branch == branch: return wt.path return None ```
## 6. PlannedPRBackend (Plan Backend - NOT a fake!)
**Location**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_store/backend.py`
**CRITICAL**: `PlanBackend` is a BACKEND, not a gateway. **Do NOT create fake implementations**. Instead, inject fake gateways into the real backend:
```python # ✅ CORRECT - Use real backend with fake gateways from erk_shared.plan_store.planned_pr import PlannedPRBackend from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues from erk_shared.gateway.time.fake import FakeTime
fake_github = FakeGitHub(prs_by_branch={...}) fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues() fake_time = FakeTime()
plan_backend = PlannedPRBackend(fake_github, fake_issues, time=fake_time) ```
**Key Method - `resolve_plan_id_for_branch`**:
From `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/objective_helpers.py`: ```python plan_id = ctx.plan_backend.resolve_plan_id_for_branch(repo_root, branch) # Returns: str (plan ID) or None if no plan for branch ```
Currently **always returns None** (plan IDs are not encoded in branch names).
## 7. objective_helpers.get_objective_for_branch Function
**Location**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/objective_helpers.py` (lines 56-73)
**Signature**: ```python def get_objective_for_branch( ctx: ErkContext, repo_root: Path, branch: str ) -> int | None: """Extract objective issue number from branch's linked plan issue.
Returns objective issue number if: 1. Branch is associated with a plan with objective_id in metadata, OR 2. Branch name encodes the objective number (fallback)
Returns None otherwise (fail-open - never blocks landing). """ try: result = ctx.plan_backend.get_plan_for_branch(repo_root, branch) except RuntimeError: return extract_objective_number(branch) if isinstance(result, PlanNotFound): return extract_objective_number(branch) if result.objective_id is not None: return result.objective_id return extract_objective_number(branch) ```
**Test Example** from `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/land/test_get_objective_for_branch.py`: ```python def test_extracts_objective_from_plnd_branch(tmp_path: Path) -> None: """plnd/ branch with O-prefix — extract objective from branch name.""" issues_ops = FakeGitHubIssues(username="testuser", issues={}) ctx = context_for_test(issues=issues_ops, cwd=tmp_path)
result = get_objective_for_branch(ctx, tmp_path, "plnd/O100-test-feature-01-15-1430")
assert result == 100 ```
## 8. CliRunner Usage Pattern
**Complete Test Example** from `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/test_wt_checkout.py`:
```python def test_checkout_named_worktree() -> None: """Test navigating to an existing worktree by name using 'co' alias.""" runner = CliRunner() with erk_inmem_env(runner) as env: # Set up worktrees: root on main, feature-work on feature-1 worktree_path = env.repo.worktrees_dir / "feature-work"
git_ops = FakeGit( worktrees={ env.cwd: [ WorktreeInfo(path=env.cwd, branch="main", is_root=True), WorktreeInfo(path=worktree_path, branch="feature-1", is_root=False), ] }, current_branches={env.cwd: "main", worktree_path: "feature-1"}, default_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir, worktree_path: env.git_dir}, )
test_ctx = env.build_context(git=git_ops)
# Act: Navigate to feature-work worktree using 'co' alias result = runner.invoke( cli, ["wt", "co", "feature-work", "--script"], obj=test_ctx, catch_exceptions=False, )
# Assert: Command succeeded assert result.exit_code == 0
# Assert: Script path is in stdout script_path = Path(result.stdout.strip()) script_content = env.script_writer.get_script_content(script_path) assert script_content is not None
# Assert: Script contains cd to worktree assert str(worktree_path) in script_content ```
## 9. Complete Land Command Test Example
From `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/land/test_core.py` (lines 31-140):
```python def test_land_outputs_deferred_execution_script() -> None: """Test land validation phase outputs script with deferred execution.""" runner = CliRunner() with erk_inmem_env(runner) as env: repo_dir = env.setup_repo_structure()
git_ops = FakeGit( worktrees=env.build_worktrees("main", ["feature-1"], repo_dir=repo_dir), current_branches={env.cwd: "feature-1"}, default_branches={env.cwd: "main"}, git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir}, repository_roots={env.cwd: env.cwd}, file_statuses={env.cwd: ([], [], [])}, )
graphite_ops = FakeGraphite( branches={ "main": BranchMetadata.trunk("main", children=["feature-1"], commit_sha="abc123"), "feature-1": BranchMetadata.branch("feature-1", "main", commit_sha="def456"), } )
github_ops = FakeGitHub( prs_by_branch={ "feature-1": PRDetails( number=123, url="https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123", title="Feature 1", body="PR body", state="OPEN", is_draft=False, base_ref_name="main", head_ref_name="feature-1", is_cross_repository=False, mergeable="MERGEABLE", merge_state_status="CLEAN", owner="owner", repo="repo", ) }, merge_should_succeed=True, )
issues_ops = FakeGitHubIssues(username="testuser")
repo = RepoContext( root=env.cwd, repo_name=env.cwd.name, repo_dir=repo_dir, worktrees_dir=repo_dir / "worktrees", pool_json_path=repo_dir / "pool.json", )
test_ctx = env.build_context( git=git_ops, graphite=graphite_ops, github=github_ops, repo=repo, use_graphite=True, issues=issues_ops, )
# Phase 1: Validation - outputs script, does NOT merge result = runner.invoke( cli, ["land", "--script", "--force", "--down"], obj=test_ctx, catch_exceptions=False )
assert result.exit_code == 0
# Verify PR was NOT merged yet (deferred to script execution) assert 123 not in github_ops.merged_prs
# Verify script was written with execution command script_path = Path(result.stdout.strip()) script_content = env.script_writer.get_script_content(script_path) assert script_content is not None assert "erk exec land-execute" in script_content ```
## 10. Key Testing Patterns Summary
### Pattern 1: Use `env.build_context()` helper ```python # ✅ CORRECT test_ctx = env.build_context(git=git_ops, github=github_ops)
# ❌ WRONG - Manual GlobalConfig construction global_config = GlobalConfig(erks_root=..., use_graphite=..., ...) test_ctx = context_for_test(git=git_ops, global_config=global_config, ...) ```
### Pattern 2: Set up branch sync info for reconcile tests ```python git_ops = FakeGit( branch_sync_info={ repo_root: { "feature-1": BranchSyncInfo( tracking_branch="origin/feature-1", ahead=2, # Local has 2 commits ahead behind=3, # Local is 3 commits behind commit_authors=["alice", "bob", "charlie"] ) } }, local_branches={repo_root: ["main", "feature-1"]}, remote_branches={repo_root: ["origin/main", "origin/feature-1"]}, ) ```
### Pattern 3: Mutation tracking assertions ```python # FakeGit exposes read-only mutation tracking properties assert "feature-1" in git_ops.branch_ops.deleted_branches assert (env.cwd, "new-branch") in git_ops.branch_ops.checked_out_branches assert len(git_ops.worktree.added_worktrees) == 1
# FakeGitHub exposes mutation tracking assert 123 in github_ops.merged_prs assert len(github_ops.created_prs) == 1 ```
### Pattern 4: Test organization - Place tests in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/reconcile/` - Use plain functions, not test classes (unless testing a class itself) - Use descriptive test names: `test_reconcile_diverged_branch_prompts_resolution()`
---
This infrastructure gives you everything needed to write comprehensive tests for `erk reconcile` with proper fake injection, mutation tracking, and assertion patterns.
